", "The unemployment rate of the most recent set of graduates is pretty high. Of those employed, the median income is dropping, and many of these jobs aren't permanent positions. And for the record, I'd rather pay higher taxes for free college than see more of you unemployed leeches."', '"Hi @sienna-stress, let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic. Your attitude is exactly why people are struggling. Let's focus on finding solutions, not blaming the victims."', '"I appreciate the thoughtful response from @great-lighting. Let's continue to foster a positive and constructive conversation about the merits and drawbacks of free college. ['That only holds true up to a certain extent. If you are a German in the 40s you don't get to say "well I don't really agree with the genocide portion of Hitler's platform, but just look what he's done for the economy! Just because I support Hitler doesn't mean I'm pro-genocide!" If you know what he's doing and still support him you're a bad person even if you don't agree with the bad thing. ', 'I've pretty frequently held a minority viewpoint on issues like *Citizens United*, and not been chastised for it. Please don't mistake "consensus" and "a group of people having considered the issues coming to a conclusion" as some kind of closed-mindedness or "echo chamber."

>the severe toxicity that Reddit expresses towards anyone who claims to have voted for Trump, or shows anything but anger towards Trump supporters is belittled and basically cast out

Yep, in the same way that someone ac', '"I'm so tired of people acting like they're victims just because they support a controversial figure. @yellow-loop, I agree with you that having a minority viewpoint doesn't mean you're wrong, but when it comes to someone like Trump, who has consistently shown disregard for human rights and decency, it's not just about having a different opinion. It's about enabling and condoning harmful behavior. And @bright-asset is right, if you support someone who is actively working to harm certain groups of people, you can't just distance yourself from those actions and claim you're a good person. You need to take responsibility for the consequences of your actions."', '"Let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. @rubbery-skipper, your point about enabling and condoning harmful behavior is a valid one. @yellow-loop, your distinction between consensus and closed-mindedness is also important to consider. Let's try to understand each other's perspectives without making assumptions or personal attacks."', '"I appreciate @moderator's efforts to keep the discussion civil, but I have to agree with @rubbery-skipper on this one. ["You do realize that if I was in the UK and said 'All Muslims are bad.'  I have committed a  crime and could be fined/jailed for violating their hate speech laws?

Specifically the Racial and Religious Hatred Act 2006 is of great concern.

It is wholly unnecessary and open to abuse/misuse.", "I'm from the UK what exactly do you think is so restrictive? I think it's important to distinguish why these laws are in place as well, in the US you practice freedom *of* religion whereas in Europe we practic", '"@grandioso-garden, you're just a snowflake who can't handle a little truth. ["> I also don't see how loyalty cannot be bought and paid for. If a soldier is making an outlandish salary, I don't think their origins will be very important.

You couldn't pay me to harass, spy on, imprison, or kill my family and relatives. It's not happening. There's no amount of money you could reasonably pay to change my mind.", 'Unlike in other countries, the military does not come from a special class of people, it is made up of volunteer citizens and is actually a pretty good representation of racial and social classes in the country.

['\> Why is the onus on the creator of the material to justify anything?

Because the creator (or adapter) of the material is trying to connect with an audience, and that audience has both demands and expectations.  In the U.S. and Europe, that audience is multi-racial and has come to expect that they will see themselves represented in some way in popular entertainment.

\> Why should art reflect American demographics? 

I would dispute that ethnic diversity is a uniquely American phenomenon', 'I'm going to come at this from a different angle, and I may take a while to get to the point, so bear with me.

I took my sister to see Fellowship of the Ring in the theater when it came out, and she complained the whole time about how white it was.  In fact, when Aragorn made his speech to Boromir about how he would "defend the white city," she leaned over to me and whispered "yeah, you and David Duke."
